heading: Research program on security of computer systems
label: 278h Research program on security of computer systems

children.text: include multidisciplinary, long-term research;

include research directed toward addressing needs identified through the activities of the Computer System Security and Privacy Advisory Board under section 278g–3(f) of this title; and12So in original. Probably should be “Information Security”.1See References in Text note below.2

promote the development of a robust research community working at the leading edge of knowledge in subject areas relevant to the security of computer systems by providing support for graduate students, post-doctoral researchers, and senior researchers.

children.heading: Post-doctoral research fellowships
children.label: 1 Post-doctoral research fellowships
children.text: The Director is authorized to establish a program to award post-doctoral research fellowships to individuals who are citizens, nationals, or lawfully admitted permanent resident aliens of the United States and are seeking research positions at institutions, including the Institute, engaged in research activities related to the security of computer systems, including the research areas described in .section 7403(a)(1) of this title

children.heading: Senior research fellowships
children.label: 2 Senior research fellowships
children.text: The Director is authorized to establish a program to award senior research fellowships to individuals seeking research positions at institutions, including the Institute, engaged in research activities related to the security of computer systems, including the research areas described in . Senior research fellowships shall be made available for established researchers at institutions of higher education who seek to change research fields and pursue studies related to the security of computer systems.section 7403(a)(1) of this title

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: To be eligible for an award under this subsection, an individual shall submit an application to the Director at such time, in such manner, and containing such information as the Director may require.

children.heading: Stipends
children.label: B Stipends
children.text: Under this subsection, the Director is authorized to provide stipends for post-doctoral research fellowships at the level of the Institute’s Post Doctoral Research Fellowship Program and senior research fellowships at levels consistent with support for a faculty member in a sabbatical position.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The Director is authorized to award grants or cooperative agreements to institutions of higher education to carry out the program established under subsection (a). No funds made available under this section shall be made available directly to any for-profit partners.

children.label: A
children.text: the number of graduate students anticipated to participate in the research project and the level of support to be provided to each;

children.label: B
children.text: the number of post-doctoral research positions included under the research project and the level of support to be provided to each;

children.label: C
children.text: the number of individuals, if any, intending to change research fields and pursue studies related to the security of computer systems to be included under the research project and the level of support to be provided to each; and

children.label: D
children.text: how the for-profit entities, nonprofit research institutions, and any other partners will participate in developing and carrying out the research and education agenda of the partnership.

children.heading: Management
children.label: 1 Management
children.text: The program established under subsection (a) shall be managed by individuals who shall have both expertise in research related to the security of computer systems and knowledge of the vulnerabilities of existing computer systems. The Director shall designate such individuals as program managers.

children.heading: Managers may be employees
children.label: 2 Managers may be employees
children.text: Program managers designated under paragraph (1) may be new or existing employees of the Institute or individuals on assignment at the Institute under the Intergovernmental Personnel Act of 1970 [ et seq.], except that individuals on assignment at the Institute under the Intergovernmental Personnel Act of 1970 shall not directly manage such employees.42 U.S.C. 4701

children.label: A
children.text: establishing and publicizing the broad research goals for the program;

children.label: B
children.text: soliciting applications for specific research projects to address the goals developed under subparagraph (A);

children.label: i
children.text: the novelty and scientific and technical merit of the proposed projects;

children.label: ii
children.text: the demonstrated capabilities of the individual or individuals submitting the applications to successfully carry out the proposed research;

children.label: iii
children.text: the impact the proposed projects will have on increasing the number of computer security researchers;

children.label: iv
children.text: the nature of the participation by for-profit entities and the extent to which the proposed projects address the concerns of industry; and

children.label: v
children.text: other criteria determined by the Director, based on information specified for inclusion in applications under subsection (c); and

children.label: D
children.text: monitoring the progress of research projects supported under the program.

children.heading: Reports
children.label: 4 Reports
children.text: The Director shall report to the Senate Committee on Commerce, Science, and Transportation and the House of Representatives Committee on Science annually on the use and responsibility of individuals on assignment at the Institute under the Intergovernmental Personnel Act of 1970 [ et seq.] who are performing duties under subsection (d).42 U.S.C. 4701

children.heading: Periodic review
children.label: 1 Periodic review
children.text: The Director shall periodically review the portfolio of research awards monitored by each program manager designated in accordance with subsection (d). In conducting those reviews, the Director shall seek the advice of the Computer System Security and Privacy Advisory Board, established under , on the appropriateness of the research goals and on the quality and utility of research projects managed by program managers in accordance with subsection (d).1section 278g–4 of this title

children.heading: Comprehensive 5-year review
children.label: 2 Comprehensive 5-year review
children.text: The Director shall also contract with the National Research Council for a comprehensive review of the program established under subsection (a) during the 5th year of the program. Such review shall include an assessment of the scientific quality of the research conducted, the relevance of the research results obtained to the goals of the program established under subsection (d)(3)(A), and the progress of the program in promoting the development of a substantial academic research community working at the leading edge of knowledge in the field. The Director shall submit to Congress a report on the results of the review under this paragraph no later than 6 years after the initiation of the program.

children.heading: Computer system
children.label: 1 Computer system
children.text: The term “computer system” has the meaning given that term in section 278g–3(d)(1) of this title.2

children.heading: Institution of higher education
children.label: 2 Institution of higher education
children.text: The term “institution of higher education” has the meaning given that term in .section 1001(a) of title 20
